lines: Breathe in through your nose... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] And out through your mouth... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five.

In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five.

In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five.

One more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] And release... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five.

Good. [short pause] Feel your chest rise and fall. [short pause] That rhythm has been with you since your first breath. [short pause] It didn't stop during the worst day of your life. [short pause] It's still going. [short pause] You're still here.
James, writing to early Christians scattered by persecution... [short pause] [short pause] people who had lost homes, safety, community... [short pause] [short pause] wrote this:

"Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. [short pause] Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything." James 1, verses 2 through 4.

Consider it joy. [short pause] That's a strange thing to say to people who are suffering. [short pause] But notice... [short pause] [short pause] James doesn't say the trial is joyful. [short pause] He says consider it joy... [short pause] [short pause] because of what it produces. [short pause] There's a chain here. [short pause] Trial produces perseverance. [short pause] Perseverance produces maturity. [short pause] Maturity produces completeness. [short pause] Each link forged under pressure.
Not masochistic joy. [short pause] Not "smile through the pain." This is the settled recognition that difficulty is doing something in you... [short pause] [short pause] not just to you.
And then Romans 8, verse 28: "We know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose."

Not "all things are good." Paul is not saying your suffering was a gift. [short pause] He's saying God's capacity to bring good from wreckage is beyond what we can see in the moment. [short pause] Some things just hurt. [short pause] Full stop. [short pause] And God works within that hurt.

Now... [short pause] [short pause] think of a challenge you've already come through. [short pause] Not the worst thing... [short pause] [short pause] not something that might overwhelm you right now. [short pause] Just something that was hard, and you made it to the other side.

What did it build in you? [short pause] Maybe patience you didn't have before. [short pause] Maybe empathy for others in similar pain. [short pause] Maybe a quiet strength you didn't know was there. [short pause] Maybe just the stubborn knowledge that you can survive more than you thought.

Put your hand on your chest. [short pause] Feel your heartbeat. [short pause] That heart has weathered every storm you've faced so far. [short pause] Every loss. [short pause] Every disappointment. [short pause] Every season that felt like it would never end. [short pause] And it's still beating. [short pause] Steady. [short pause] Faithful. [short pause] That's not nothing. [short pause] That's evidence.
Now... [short pause] [short pause] what's the hard thing on your plate right now? [short pause] You don't have to solve it. [short pause] Just name it.
Here's a reframe. [short pause] Not toxic positivity. [short pause] Not pretending it's fine. [short pause] Just a shift in angle.
Instead of "Why is this happening to me?"... [short pause] [short pause] try "What is this asking of me?"
Instead of "I can't handle this"... [short pause] [short pause] try "I haven't handled this yet."
These aren't word games. [short pause] They're the difference between a fixed story and an unfinished one. [short pause] You are in the middle, not at the end.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] some things just hurt. [short pause] I'm not going to pretend otherwise, and I don't think you want me to. [short pause] But I trust that you work in all things... [short pause] [short pause] even the ones that don't make sense yet. [short pause] Especially those. [short pause] Give me perseverance today. [short pause] Not the gritting-my-teeth kind. [short pause] The kind that comes from knowing you are present in the struggle, not just waiting at the finish line. [short pause] Hold me steady. [short pause] Amen.

You are not fragile. [short pause] You are being shaped. [short pause] And shaping is slow, and it's uncomfortable, and it doesn't always feel like progress. [short pause] But the chain James describes... [short pause] [short pause] trials, perseverance, maturity, completeness... [short pause] [short pause] that chain is forging in you right now.

So meet today. [short pause] Not with denial, not with dread. [short pause] With the steady knowledge that you have survived before, you are held now, and you are becoming something you couldn't become any other way.
May God give you endurance for what's hard and eyes to see what it's building.
